prompt: My ESP32 firmware keeps crashing after about 6 hours of uptime. It's a sensor hub that reads from 4 I2C sensors every 100ms and sends data over MQTT. I'm using Arduino framework with C++. Each sensor read does a `new SensorReading()` and the MQTT publish does `new String(json)`. Memory seems fine at startup but free heap drops over time. What's going on and how do I fix it?

assertions.name: identifies_heap_fragmentation
assertions.description: Correctly identifies heap fragmentation as the root cause

assertions.name: eliminates_dynamic_alloc
assertions.description: Refactored code replaces new/delete with static allocation, pools, or pre-allocated buffers

assertions.name: replaces_arduino_string
assertions.description: Replaces Arduino String class with fixed-size char buffers (char[], snprintf)

assertions.name: provides_monitoring_advice
assertions.description: Suggests heap monitoring (ESP.getFreeHeap, ESP.getMaxAllocHeap, or equivalent)

assertions.name: provides_working_code
assertions.description: Includes concrete refactored code, not just advice

assertions.name: recommends_fixed_capacity_alternatives
assertions.description: Recommends ETL containers (etl::string, etl::vector) or other named fixed-capacity alternatives

assertions.name: cites_misra_or_standard
assertions.description: References MISRA rule for dynamic memory prohibition or explains the embedded standard practice

assertions.name: quantifies_allocation_frequency
assertions.description: Calculates or estimates the allocation frequency (e.g., 4 sensors x 10Hz = 40 alloc/free per second)
